quiz.title: Geometry: Applications of Trigonometry — Angle of Depression
quiz.subtitle: Unit 8 Quiz 9: Real-World Problems Using the Angle of Depression (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Geometry: 8.9 Applications of Trigonometry (Angle of Depression)

questions.question: An observer at the top of a cliff looks down at a boat. The angle of depression is measured from:
options: The horizontal line from the observer downward to the line of sight
The vertical line downward to the boat
The line of sight upward from the boat
The base of the cliff upward to the observer
questions.explanation: The angle of depression is measured from the horizontal (a line parallel to the ground) downward to the line of sight when looking at an object below. By alternate interior angles, the angle of depression equals the angle of elevation from the object to the observer.

questions.question: A park ranger stands on a cliff 200 feet above a lake. She spots a boat at an angle of depression of 25°. How far is the boat from the base of the cliff, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: The angle of depression (25°) equals the angle of elevation from the boat to the ranger. Use tangent: tan(25°) = 200 / d. With a calculator, tan(25°) ≈ 0.4663. So d = 200 / 0.4663 ≈ 428.9 feet.

questions.question: A lighthouse keeper is 120 feet above the water. She spots a ship at an angle of depression of 18°. What is the direct line-of-sight distance from the keeper to the ship, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: Use sine with the angle of elevation from the ship (18°): sin(18°) = 120 / L. With a calculator, sin(18°) ≈ 0.3090. So L = 120 / 0.3090 ≈ 388.3 feet.

questions.question: A pilot in a plane spots a landmark on the ground at an angle of depression of 30°. The plane is directly above a point that is 5,000 feet horizontally from the landmark. What is the plane's altitude, rounded to the nearest foot?
questions.explanation: The angle of depression (30°) equals the angle of elevation from the landmark to the plane. Use tangent: tan(30°) = h / 5000. Since tan(30°) = 1/√3 ≈ 0.5774, we have h = 5000 / √3 ≈ 2886.8 ≈ 2,887 feet.

questions.question: A security camera is mounted on a building 60 feet above the ground. It records a person 150 feet from the base of the building. What is the angle of depression from the camera to the person, rounded to the nearest tenth of a degree?
questions.explanation: The angle of depression equals the angle of elevation from the person to the camera. Use tangent: tan(θ) = 60 / 150 = 0.4. With a calculator, θ = tan⁻¹(0.4) ≈ 21.80° ≈ 21.8°.

questions.question: A rescue helicopter hovers at an altitude of 800 feet. The crew spots a stranded hiker at an angle of depression of 40°. What is the horizontal distance from the helicopter to the hiker, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: Use tangent: tan(40°) = 800 / d. With a calculator, tan(40°) ≈ 0.8391. So d = 800 / 0.8391 ≈ 953.4 feet.

questions.question: A person looks out a window 45 feet above the ground and sees a parked car at an angle of depression of 22°. How far is the car from the base of the building, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: Use tangent: tan(22°) = 45 / d. With a calculator, tan(22°) ≈ 0.4040. So d = 45 / 0.4040 ≈ 111.39 ≈ 111.4 feet.

questions.question: A bridge is 180 feet above a river. A person on the bridge sees a buoy at an angle of depression of 12°. What is the direct line-of-sight distance from the person to the buoy, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: Use sine with the angle of elevation from the buoy (12°): sin(12°) = 180 / L. With a calculator, sin(12°) ≈ 0.2079. So L = 180 / 0.2079 ≈ 865.8 feet.

questions.question: A pilot flying at 20,000 feet sees the airport at an angle of depression of 15°. What is the direct line-of-sight distance from the plane to the airport, rounded to the nearest foot?
questions.explanation: Use sine with the angle of elevation from the airport (15°): sin(15°) = 20,000 / L. With a calculator, sin(15°) ≈ 0.2588. So L = 20,000 / 0.2588 ≈ 77,274 feet (about 14.6 miles).

questions.question: From the top of a cliff 300 feet high, a ranger sees two boats in a straight line from the base of the cliff. The angle of depression to the nearer boat is 40° and to the farther boat is 25°. What is the distance between the two boats, rounded to the nearest tenth of a foot?
questions.explanation: For the nearer boat: d₁ = 300 / tan(40°) ≈ 300 / 0.8391 = 357.5 feet. For the farther boat: d₂ = 300 / tan(25°) ≈ 300 / 0.4663 = 643.4 feet. The distance between boats is d₂ - d₁ = 643.4 - 357.5 = 285.9 feet.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered angle of depression problems. You understand that the angle of depression equals the angle of elevation and can set up the correct trigonometric relationship every time.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember: the angle of depression is measured from the horizontal downward. By alternate interior angles, it equals the angle of elevation from the object below. Draw the right triangle carefully.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: (1) drawing the horizontal line from the observer, (2) recognizing that the angle of depression equals the angle of elevation inside the triangle, (3) using tangent for height/distance, sine or cosine when the line of sight is involved.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The angle of depression is the angle between the horizontal and the downward line of sight. The key insight: this angle equals the angle of elevation from the object to the observer (alternate interior angles). Use SOH CAH TOA to set up your equation.
